heading: Quinyambie vs Racecourse Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Quinyambie, South Australia and Racecourse Bay, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Quinyambie has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Racecourse Bay (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Quinyambie or Racecourse Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Quinyambie has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Racecourse Bay scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Quinyambie scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Racecourse Bay scores 39/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Quinyambie or Racecourse Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Quinyambie has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
